transformation_type: electrophilic aromatic formylation of activated arenes
mechanism_type: Lewis-acid-mediated introduction of CHO groups using CO/HCl or cyanide-derived formylation equivalents
common_conditions: activate the aromatic substrate under strongly Lewis-acidic conditions and generate a formylating species from CO/HCl or a cyanide-derived equivalent to furnish an aromatic aldehyde
description_short: Classical aromatic formylation family using either carbon monoxide/hydrogen chloride or cyanide-derived formylation equivalents under Lewis-acidic conditions.

transformation_type: oxidative coupling of terminal alkynes to diynes
mechanism_type: copper-mediated oxidative dimerization or heterocoupling through copper acetylides and aerobic oxidation
common_conditions: form copper acetylides from terminal alkynes, then oxidize under aerobic or copper(II)-mediated conditions to obtain symmetrical or unsymmetrical 1,3-diynes
description_short: Oxidative alkyne-coupling family widely used for diyne synthesis, macrocyclization, and heterocoupling variants in complex-molecule synthesis.

transformation_type: nucleophilic carbon-carbon bond formation with organomagnesium reagents
mechanism_type: formation of organomagnesium halides followed by addition to electrophiles
common_conditions: prepare the organomagnesium reagent from an organohalide and magnesium in dry ethereal solvent, then add to carbonyls, nitriles, carbon dioxide, or related electrophiles before aqueous workup
description_short: Foundational organometallic addition chemistry in which organomagnesium reagents act as strongly nucleophilic carbon equivalents toward diverse electrophiles.

transformation_type: stereoelectronically controlled fragmentation to unsaturated products
mechanism_type: concerted or stepwise ionic beta-elimination/fragmentation with aligned electrofuge and nucleofuge
common_conditions: activate a suitably substituted precursor so that aligned electrofugal and nucleofugal groups fragment to generate an alkene, carbonyl, or ring-opened product
description_short: Fragmentation family that cleaves rigid, properly aligned precursors into unsaturated fragments under strong-base, alkoxide, or related triggering conditions.

transformation_type: asymmetric intramolecular aldol annulation to bicyclic enones
mechanism_type: amino-acid-catalyzed Michael addition and intramolecular aldol cyclization of 1,3-dione-derived systems
common_conditions: combine a cyclopentane-1,3-dione or cyclohexane-1,3-dione derivative with an enone, then induce asymmetric amino-acid-catalyzed annulation and dehydration to the bicyclic enone
description_short: Asymmetric amino-acid-catalyzed annulation that generates optically enriched bicyclic enones central to steroid and terpenoid synthesis.
